title: 顺水推舟
summary:
At a family gathering celebrating Lynn Marchmont's return, Lynn interacts with various members of the Claude and Croud families. Aunt Kathy, who is interested in séances and Egyptian prophecies, greets Lynn and discusses the effects of Lynn's time serving in Egypt. Rosalyn Croud, the widow of Gordon Croud, arrives with her brother, David Hunter. Lynn observes that while Rosalyn is beautiful, she appears unable to wear expensive clothing properly. Lynn perceives David Hunter as an arrogant, thin, and rude young man who seems reckless and unreliable.

During the gathering, David makes contemptuous remarks regarding the wealth Gordon Croud spent on his modern house in Froebank, located in Wormsley Valley. As the group moves to a dilapidated dining room for dinner, Lynn senses a palpable, intentional hostility and malice in the atmosphere. She interprets this tension as a consequence of the war's aftermath and realizes the local population seems to harbor a deep hatred toward outsiders.

Sitting beside David, Lynn engages him in conversation about this pervasive malice. David compares modern hostility to the tangible witchcraft of the Middle Ages and suggests that even if the family hated him and Rosalyn, they could do nothing about it, asserting that "nothing is safe." Lynn finds herself drawn to David's dangerous worldview. The meal is interrupted when Aunt Kathy attempts to discuss spiritualism, a topic that Dr. Lionel Claude sternly dismisses as mere superstition.
